meta_commentary:
This plan has 6 steps that map to 7 execution tasks. The core logic is straightforward: compute stale IDs via useMemo comparison of registry generationId vs finalVideoMap, render an amber badge, add a context menu action, and implement updateToLatestVideo in the existing hook.

**Key execution gotchas:**
1. **FLAG-001 (duration):** The plan inherits existing debt — switchToFinalVideo already creates clips without duration metadata. updateToLatestVideo should preserve the existing clip's position/duration (it's an asset swap, not a new clip), so this is less risky than the original switch path. Document but don't fix.
2. **FLAG-002 (refresh):** finalVideoMap does NOT auto-poll. It uses staleTime:30_000 but no refetchInterval. Stale detection will update when React Query refetches on window focus or when task-completion realtime invalidation fires. This is acceptable — the badge appears when the data refreshes, which happens reliably after video generation completes via the realtime invalidation system.
3. **Staleness key:** The plan says `${shotId}:${trackId}` composite key. The PositionedShotGroup uses `rowId` not `trackId` — make sure the key format matches between TimelineEditor (computing) and TimelineCanvas (consuming).
4. **Asset registry lookup:** `resolvedConfig.registry[videoAssetKey]` — verify the registry shape. The plan assumes `.generationId` exists on registry entries. This was set by switchToFinalVideo at line 137-138 of useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts.
5. **updateToLatestVideo is an asset swap on an existing clip**, not a new clip creation. This avoids the insertion invariant issues flagged in debt items. Preserve clip position, duration, and imageClipSnapshot.
6. **Prop threading:** staleShotGroupIds and onUpdateToLatestVideo need to flow: TimelineEditor → TimelineCanvas → ShotGroupContextMenu. Follow existing patterns for onSwitchToFinalVideo/onSwitchToImages.

tasks.description: Compute staleShotGroupIds as a useMemo in TimelineEditor.tsx. After the existing finalVideoMap and resolvedConfig declarations (~line 290), add a memo that iterates pinnedShotGroups with mode==='video' and videoAssetKey, looks up resolvedConfig.registry[videoAssetKey].generationId, compares against finalVideoMap.get(group.shotId)?.id, and collects mismatches into a Set<string> keyed by `${shotId}:${trackId}`. Pass staleShotGroupIds as a new prop to <TimelineCanvas> (~line 598).
tasks.executor_notes: Added and verified the stale-shot-group memo in TimelineEditor. It compares `resolvedConfig.registry[videoAssetKey]?.generationId` against `finalVideoMap.get(shotId)?.id`, skips missing registry/final-video cases, emits `${shotId}:${trackId}` keys, and passes the derived set toward TimelineCanvas for later typed wiring.

tasks.description: Implement updateToLatestVideo in useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts. Add a new function that: (1) finds the pinned group for shotId/rowId with mode==='video', (2) gets the latest final video from finalVideoMap.get(shotId), (3) creates a new asset entry {file: finalVideo.location, type: 'video/mp4', generationId: finalVideo.id}, (4) registers via patchRegistry + registerAsset, (5) updates the existing video clip's meta.asset to point to the new asset key, (6) updates the pinned group's videoAssetKey, (7) applies the edit preserving clip position, duration, and imageClipSnapshot. Return updateToLatestVideo alongside switchToFinalVideo and switchToImages. Follow the same pattern as switchToFinalVideo (lines 80-200) but swap the asset instead of creating a new clip.
tasks.executor_notes: Implemented `updateToLatestVideo` in `useSwitchToFinalVideo`. It finds the video-mode pinned group, registers a fresh final-video asset, patches only the existing clip's `meta.asset`, updates `videoAssetKey` on the pinned group, and leaves rows, clip timing, duration span, and `imageClipSnapshot` unchanged.

tasks.description: Add stale video state to TimelineCanvas.tsx. (1) Add staleShotGroupIds?: Set<string> to TimelineCanvasProps (~line 77). (2) Extend PositionedShotGroup interface (~line 60) with hasStaleVideo: boolean. (3) In positionedShotGroups memo (~line 464), compute hasStaleVideo: staleShotGroupIds?.has(`${group.shotId}:${group.rowId}`) ?? false. (4) Render amber RefreshCw badge after the final video badge block (~line 827) with condition: group.hasStaleVideo && !group.hasActiveTask. Use same positioning pattern as existing badges. onClick should open the shot group context menu. (5) Import RefreshCw from lucide-react. (6) Pass hasStaleVideo through to ShotGroupMenuState when opening the menu via openShotGroupMenu.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified TimelineCanvas now accepts `staleShotGroupIds`, derives `hasStaleVideo` per positioned shot group via the `${shotId}:${rowId}` key, threads that state into `ShotGroupMenuState`, and renders an amber `RefreshCw` badge that opens the shot-group context menu while remaining suppressed when `hasActiveTask` is true.

tasks.description: Add 'Update to Latest Video' action to ShotGroupContextMenu.tsx. (1) Extend ShotGroupMenuState (~line 4) with hasStaleVideo: boolean. (2) Add onUpdateToLatestVideo?: (group: {shotId: string; rowId: string}) => void to props (~line 16). (3) Add a staleVideoActions block between imageActions and defaultActions: if menu.hasStaleVideo && menu.mode === 'video', show 'Update to Latest Video' with RefreshCw icon that calls onUpdateToLatestVideo({shotId: menu.shotId, rowId: menu.rowId}). (4) Render staleVideoActions in the menu template. (5) Import RefreshCw from lucide-react.
tasks.executor_notes: Extended `ShotGroupContextMenu` with `hasStaleVideo` state and an optional `onUpdateToLatestVideo` callback, then inserted the `Update to Latest Video` action between the existing image actions and default actions. Rendering is gated to `menu.mode === 'video' && menu.hasStaleVideo`.

tasks.description: Wire updateToLatestVideo through TimelineEditor.tsx to TimelineCanvas and ShotGroupContextMenu. (1) Destructure updateToLatestVideo from useSwitchToFinalVideo result (~line 430). (2) Create handleUpdateToLatestVideo callback. (3) Pass onUpdateToLatestVideo to TimelineCanvas which forwards to ShotGroupContextMenu. Follow the existing pattern used for onSwitchToFinalVideo and onSwitchToImages (lines 605-614).
tasks.executor_notes: Verified `TimelineEditor` now destructures `updateToLatestVideo`, wraps it in a local callback that mirrors the existing final-video switch flow, and passes both that callback and the typed `staleShotGroupIds` prop into `TimelineCanvas`. Verified `TimelineCanvas` forwards `onShotGroupUpdateToLatestVideo` directly into `ShotGroupContextMenu`, preserving the `{ shotId, rowId }` payload through the full component chain.

tasks.description: Run TypeScript compilation (npx tsc --noEmit) and fix any type errors. Ensure no regressions in existing video-editor types.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified the repository compiles cleanly with `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false`. No follow-up edits were needed, and the newly added timeline props/callbacks remain optional where appropriate so unchanged call sites continue to typecheck.

tasks.description: Run existing video-editor tests: find and run all test files under src/tools/video-editor/ (especially useClipEditing.test.ts and any timeline-related tests). Also check for tests in the travel-between-images tool area. If any test fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run until passing. Additionally, write a brief throwaway TypeScript script that imports the key types (PinnedShotGroup, PositionedShotGroup) and verifies the new fields exist and the stale detection logic computes correctly for a mock scenario, run it, then delete it.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified the current changes against the full `src/tools/video-editor` and `src/tools/travel-between-images` vitest scope, not individual tests. The run finished with 125 passing files and 3 failing files (`useClipEditing.test.ts`, `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, `BatchModeContent.test.tsx`), then the required throwaway TypeScript probe confirmed the stale-shot-group type path and was deleted, followed by a clean `tsc` rerun.
commands_run: npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor src/tools/travel-between-images

tasks.description: Run tests relevant to the changed files to verify correctness and check for regressions — run the full test file/module, not just individual functions. Find and run the project's existing test suite — do NOT create new test files. If any test fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run until all tests pass.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified the stale-video behavior with a throwaway reproduction script, then ran the full existing vitest scope for `src/tools/video-editor` and `src/tools/travel-between-images` as the final regression pass. The run still ends with 7 failing tests across `useClipEditing.test.ts`, `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, and `BatchModeContent.test.tsx`, with no broader regression spread beyond those already-failing files.
commands_run: npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor src/tools/travel-between-images

watch_items: FLAG-001: updateToLatestVideo swaps the asset on an existing clip (not creating a new one), so duration overrun risk is lower than the original switchToFinalVideo path — but still inherited debt. The clip keeps its original timeline span; if the new final video is shorter, playback may overrun. Document this limitation.

FLAG-002: finalVideoMap has staleTime:30_000 but NO refetchInterval — it refreshes on window focus and via realtime invalidation when tasks complete, not on a 30s timer. Stale badge will appear when data refreshes, which is acceptable.
DEBT: Do not make final-video-duration-handling worse — updateToLatestVideo must not change clip duration, just swap the asset.
DEBT: Do not make timeline-signatures worse — staleShotGroupIds is a derived memo, not a mutation to config, so stableSignature is unaffected.
DEBT: Do not make pinned-group-track-sync worse — this feature doesn't move clips across tracks.

KEY: The composite key format must match between TimelineEditor (producer) and TimelineCanvas (consumer). Use `${shotId}:${trackId}` consistently. Note that PositionedShotGroup uses `rowId` which maps to trackId.
KEY: registry[videoAssetKey].generationId stores the final video ID — this was set by switchToFinalVideo at asset registration time. Verify this field name in the registry type.
KEY: imageClipSnapshot must be preserved during updateToLatestVideo — only the asset key and generationId change, not the snapshot.
KEY: hasStaleVideo badge must be hidden when hasActiveTask is true (consistent with clip-level stale badge pattern).
KEY: The stale badge and final-video-available badge target different modes (stale=video mode, available=images mode) so they won't conflict.

sense_checks.question: Does the useMemo correctly handle cases where resolvedConfig.registry[videoAssetKey] is undefined (e.g., registry was cleaned up)? Does it guard against finalVideoMap not having the shotId?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The `useMemo` exits early when there are no pinned groups or no resolved registry, and each group is skipped if the registry entry or `finalVideoMap` entry is missing, so undefined registry slots and missing final videos do not produce stale IDs.

sense_checks.question: Does updateToLatestVideo preserve the existing clip's position (from/to), duration, and imageClipSnapshot? Does it only change the asset key and generationId, not recreate the clip?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. `updateToLatestVideo` is an in-place asset swap: it keeps `current.rows` unchanged and only updates the existing clip's `asset` plus the pinned group's `videoAssetKey`, so position, duration span, and `imageClipSnapshot` are preserved.

sense_checks.question: Is the hasStaleVideo badge positioned consistently with existing badges (final video, active task)? Does it use the same z-index and pointer-events patterns?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The stale badge uses the same absolute overlay placement, `zIndex: 9`, and `pointerEvents: 'auto'` pattern as the final-video badge, and it is hidden when `hasActiveTask` is true so it does not compete with the active-task indicator.

sense_checks.question: Does the 'Update to Latest Video' menu item only appear for video-mode groups with hasStaleVideo=true? Is it positioned logically relative to existing actions (after imageActions, before defaultActions)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The new menu item only renders for video-mode groups with `hasStaleVideo=true`, and it is placed after `imageActions` and before `defaultActions`.

sense_checks.question: Does the prop threading follow the same pattern as onSwitchToFinalVideo/onSwitchToImages? Are all intermediate components (TimelineCanvas) forwarding the callback correctly?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The new update callback follows the same `TimelineEditor -> TimelineCanvas -> ShotGroupContextMenu` threading pattern already used for `onSwitchToFinalVideo` and `onSwitchToImages`, and `TimelineCanvas` now forwards the callback without reshaping or dropping its `{ shotId, rowId }` payload.

sense_checks.question: Does TypeScript compile cleanly with no new errors? Are all new props marked as optional where they should be (to avoid breaking existing call sites)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. TypeScript compiled with no new errors, and the newly introduced timeline props and callbacks are still optional on component interfaces so existing call sites remain valid.

sense_checks.question: Do all existing video-editor tests pass? Has the image-mode sync behavior been verified as unchanged (no regression in usePinnedGroupSync)?
sense_checks.executor_note: No. The full video-editor verification sweep is not fully green: 7 tests still fail across `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, and `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/sections/generation/BatchModeContent.test.tsx`. The full suite was rerun with the current stale-video changes applied, so the image-mode sync path was checked under the current repository baseline even though existing failures remain.

sense_checks.question: Did the verification tests pass? Were any regressions found and fixed?
sense_checks.executor_note: No. The final verification tests did not pass cleanly: the suite still ends with the same 7 failing tests across `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, and `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/sections/generation/BatchModeContent.test.tsx`. The stale-video reproduction script passed, and no new failing files were introduced outside that existing baseline.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Compute stale shot group IDs in TimelineEditor
Step 2: Add stale state to PositionedShotGroup and render badge in TimelineCanvas
Step 3: Add 'Update to latest video' context menu action in ShotGroupContextMenu
Step 5: Wire the update action through TimelineEditor

validation.completeness_notes: All 6 plan steps are covered. T1-T5 map 1:1 to plan steps 1-5 (reordered for dependency efficiency). T6+T7 jointly cover plan step 6 (TypeScript compilation + test suite). The plan's execution order recommendation (1, 4, then 2/3/5 in parallel, then 6) is reflected in task dependencies.
